sourceMovie.title: All the Money in the World
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: All the Money in the World works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Equalizer 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If All the Money in the World clicked for you, The Equalizer 3 (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after All the Money in the World?
faq.answer: Try The Equalizer 3, A Perfect World, The Night of the Hunter—each shares DNA with All the Money in the World while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is All the Money in the World?
faq.answer: All the Money in the World (2017) is best described as Crime, History, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is All the Money in the World worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
